data.title: The Great Standstill: American Workers Stay Put As Job Switching Appetite Hits Five-Year Low Amid Uncertainty
data.description: The United States is experiencing a notable decline in job satisfaction, as reported by the New York Federal Reserve's SCE Labor Market Survey. Currently, workers are showing the lowest interest in changing employers since 2021, with only 9.7% expecting to make a move - the lowest figure in five years. Additionally, satisfaction levels related to wage compensation and opportunities for promotion have reached their lowest points since the survey's inception in 2014. The percentage of individuals actively seeking new job opportunities has also decreased, dropping to 22.5% from 23.8% in November 2025. This decline is particularly evident among individuals under 45 and female workers. The decrease in job mobility is primarily being driven by workers without a college degree. Despite this reduced willingness to change jobs, workers are demanding higher salaries, with the average reservation wage hitting a record high of $84,762 in March. For more details, refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP Trade Sideways Ahead Of Powell's Final Meeting As Fed Chair
data.description: Bitcoin was holding steady near $76,000, with major altcoins showing limited movement as traders exercised caution ahead of the Federal Open Market Committee meeting. Current cryptocurrency prices include Bitcoin (BTC) at $76,200.02, Ethereum (ETH) at $2,292.36, Solana (SOL) at $83.66, XRP at $1.38, Dogecoin (DOGE) at $0.09936, and Shiba Inu (SHIB) at $0.056116. Coinglass data displayed that 67,855 traders were liquidated in the last day, amounting to $192.38 million in losses. SoSoValue data revealed net outflows of $263.2 million from spot Bitcoin ETFs on Monday, with spot Ethereum ETFs seeing net outflows of $50.5 million. Top gainers in the past 24 hours were Humanity Protocol, siren, and Terra Classic. Notable news includes BlackRock establishing a new crypto partnership. For more information, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Michael Saylor Says Financial Giants Will Cause 'Massive Supply Shock' For Bitcoin
data.description: Three billionaire investors set ambitious Bitcoin predictions at Bitcoin 2026 in Las Vegas, with price targets ranging from $125,000 to discussing a looming supply shock. Tim Draper emphasized the importance of companies holding 5% to 15% of their treasury in Bitcoin as a hedge against fiat currency devaluation and to prepare for potential financial disruptions. He stated that it is now considered irresponsible for companies to not have some portion of their treasury in Bitcoin for protection. Draper also cautioned about the possibility of a bank run if more merchants start accepting Bitcoin exclusively, pointing to Silicon Valley Bank's collapse as an example of the vulnerabilities in the banking system.

data.title: Why Robinhood Shares Are Dropping Before Key Earnings Report
data.description: Shares of Robinhood Markets, Inc. (NASDAQ: HOOD) experienced a decline on Tuesday due to a risk-off market impacting growth stocks. Traders are closely monitoring how a potential crypto market downturn could influence the company's transaction-based results leading up to its upcoming first-quarter earnings report. Set to be released after the market closes on Tuesday, investors are particularly interested in how decreasing cryptocurrency values might have affected trading activity and revenue. In the fourth quarter, cryptocurrencies contributed to 28% of the revenue generated from transactions, making the recent drop in Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) and Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH) significant. Concerns are focused on whether the decline in prices has led to decreased user involvement and trading volumes. Additionally, market sentiment has been influenced by recent developments, such as the announcement on April 22 of Robinhood Ventures Fund I (NYSE: RVI) investing $75 million in OpenAI, marking one of its largest investments and indicating a move into frontier AI opportunities. For more information, visit the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Bitcoin's Path To $100,000 Will Be Rocky, Galaxy Digital CEO Michael Novogratz Says
data.description: Michael Novogratz, a billionaire investor, expressed doubts about Bitcoin's ability to reach $100,000 without significant changes in economic conditions, particularly in monetary policies. In a recent interview with Bloomberg, Novogratz highlighted the importance of central banks, especially the Federal Reserve, easing policies for Bitcoin to see a sustained increase. However, he cautioned that ongoing geopolitical tensions and inflation concerns may hinder policymakers from taking such actions, limiting the potential for significant growth in the near future. Bitcoin was trading around $76,000 at the time of Novogratz's remarks, significantly lower than its previous peak of $126,000. For the full story, refer to Benzinga.com.
